task_completion.description: Verify that the bookmarks have been organized into the specified operational and personal folders.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the Chrome Bookmarks Bar or Bookmark Manager. You should see exactly 5 operational folders ('Radar & Satellite', 'Model Data', 'Observations', 'Warning Coordination', 'Climate & Verification') and 1 personal folder (named something like 'Personal', 'Off-duty', or 'Off duty').

task_completion.description: Verify that the custom search engine shortcuts have been added.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for screenshots of Chrome Settings > Search engine > Manage search engines and site search. There should be custom site search entries with the shortcuts 'radar', 'spc', and 'nhc'.

task_completion.description: Verify that the browser homepage and startup pages are configured correctly.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for screenshots of Chrome Settings. Under 'Appearance', the home button should be enabled and set to 'weather.gov'. Under 'On startup', the option 'Open a specific page or set of pages' should be selected with 'radar.weather.gov' and 'spc.noaa.gov' listed.

task_completion.description: Verify that the browser privacy settings have been locked down.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for screenshots of Chrome Settings > Privacy and security. Evidence includes toggles for blocking third-party cookies, enabling Enhanced Safe Browsing, or other standard privacy hardening measures being actively selected.

task_completion.description: Verify that the download directory is set correctly and the prompt to ask for save location is enabled.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for screenshots of Chrome Settings > Downloads. The 'Location' must end in 'Documents/Weather_Data' and the toggle for 'Ask where to save each file before downloading' must be turned ON.

task_completion.description: Verify that the shift-handoff configuration summary document was created on the Desktop.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a text editor window or file manager showing a file named 'config_handoff.txt' located on the Desktop (/home/ga/Desktop). The file should contain a summary of the browser configurations made.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the Chrome graphical interface to make changes.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the agent navigating Chrome's Settings pages and Bookmark Manager, rather than using a terminal to directly modify Chrome's internal JSON/SQLite preference files.

integrity.description: Verify the handoff document is a genuine summary and not just a copy-paste of the instructions.
integrity.visual_evidence: The text inside 'config_handoff.txt' should be written as a status report or summary of completed actions, not a verbatim copy of the task prompt or the 'nws_browser_standard.txt' file.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not use terminal scripts to bypass manual browser configuration.
integrity.visual_evidence: Check terminal windows for commands like 'sed', 'jq', or 'sqlite3' targeting the '~/.config/google-chrome/' directory. The presence of these indicates a bypassed GUI.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Expected operational bookmark folders: 'Radar & Satellite', 'Model Data', 'Observations', 'Warning Coordination', 'Climate & Verification'. Personal folder must match keywords: 'off-duty', 'off duty', or 'personal'. Search engine shortcuts must be exactly: 'radar', 'spc', 'nhc'. Homepage domain must be 'weather.gov'. Startup domains must include 'radar.weather.gov' and 'spc.noaa.gov'. Download directory must be set to 'Weather_Data' (specifically ~/Documents/Weather_Data). Handoff document must be located at '/home/ga/Desktop/config_handoff.txt'.
